content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible bending rightward, and the car appears to be on course near the center-left of the track.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 80, target = (80 + 5) % 121 = 85
- Target WP85: x=1.1136, y=4.3460
- Car: x=1.4228, y=4.4070, heading=-153.48°
- dy = 4.3460 - 4.4070 = -0.0610
- dx = 1.1136 - 1.4228 = -0.3092
- bearing = atan2(-0.0610, -0.3092) → third quadrant
- angle = atan(0.0610/0.3092) = atan(0.1973) = 11.16°
- bearing = -(180° - 11.16°) = -168.84°
- delta = -168.84° - (-153.48°) = -15.36°
- |delta| ≈ 15°: waypoint is to the RIGHT, use negative steering ~-10° to -15°

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Delta of -15.4° to WP85 means the waypoint is to the right as the track curves right, so I apply -15° steering at 1.5 speed to follow the curve.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "Delta under 5° means steer 0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Delta 5-15° use ±5°; 15-30° use ±10 to ±15°; 30-50° use ±15 to ±20°; >50° use ±25°",
    "Near WP79-85, track curves right — expect negative delta values requiring right steering",
    "Speed 0.75 for |delta|>50°; 1.0 for 30-50°; 1.5 for <30°",
    "Car is slightly left of center (0.053m) — consistent with right curve approach",
    "Heading near -153° to -165° corresponds to westward/southwest travel on this section",
    "Track curves right after WP80 toward WP85-90 — negative delta values expected",
    "Zero steering when delta < 5° prevents oscillation and maintains speed",
    "Track width ~0.76m — small corrections are sufficient, avoid overcorrecting"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.656, 1.586, 1.506, 1.423],
    "y": [4.575, 4.530, 4.470, 4.407],
    "yaw": [-173.66, -165.88, -158.68, -153.48]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP85 at (1.114, 4.346): dy=-0.061, dx=-0.309, bearing=-168.84°, delta=-15.4° → waypoint to the RIGHT, steer -15°",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": -15.0
}
```
